name: Lincoln S.D.A.
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Seventh-day Adventist Church members, students, and employees with competitive loan and deposit products.
description_long:
Lincoln SDA Credit Union is a faith-based, member-owned credit union organized specifically to serve members of the Seventh-day Adventist Church, students and employees of Adventist colleges, and employees of Adventist hospitals. As a not-for-profit financial institution, it operates under NCUA insurance and is governed by its membership rather than external shareholders. The credit union offers a full suite of consumer financial products including auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, share accounts, and share certificates, positioning itself as a values-aligned alternative to traditional for-profit banks.

The institution provides competitive lending products across multiple categories: used vehicle loans starting at 5.95%, personal loans at 12.00%, credit cards ranging from 12.00–14.00% APR, and share certificates offering rates up to 3.25%. Members gain access to home banking through their Member.Net platform, allowing 24/7 account management. The credit union also offers automobile tools including vehicle shopping assistance and refinance calculators. Operating from a single location in Lincoln, Nebraska, it maintains traditional lobby hours and can be reached via phone at 402-489-8886 or 800-244-7168.

Lincoln SDA distinguishes itself through its explicit faith-based mission and membership restrictions, creating a community-focused institution aligned with Seventh-day Adventist values. This specialized membership base sets it apart from general-purpose credit unions and for-profit banks. The organization emphasizes member ownership and competitively priced products designed specifically for its target demographic rather than the broader public.

The primary limitation is membership eligibility—the credit union only serves members of the Seventh-day Adventist Church, Adventist college students/employees, and Adventist hospital employees, making it inaccessible to the general public. As a smaller, single-location institution, it may have fewer physical branches and less sophisticated digital infrastructure compared to larger credit unions. The website provides limited transparency on specific loan terms, eligibility requirements, and fee structures, requiring direct contact for detailed product information.
services: Share certificates (certificates of deposit up to 3.25%)
Online home banking via Member.Net platform
pros: Used vehicle loans starting at 5.95% APR—competitive rate for auto financing
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits return to members rather than external shareholders
NCUA-insured deposits provide federal backing for share accounts and certificates
24/7 online banking through Member.Net platform for account access and loan payments
Specialized service model tailored to Seventh-day Adventist community values and financial needs
Automobile tools including refinance calculator and vehicle shopping assistance
Share certificates offering rates up to 3.25% for members seeking savings vehicles
cons: Membership restricted to Seventh-day Adventist Church members, Adventist college students/employees, and Adventist hospital employees—not open to general public
Single physical location in Lincoln, Nebraska limits branch access and in-person service options
Website lacks detailed loan terms, APR ranges for different credit tiers, and fee schedules—requires phone calls for specific pricing
Limited information about credit card rewards, cashback, or benefits compared to mainstream issuers
No indication of mobile app availability or advanced digital banking features
best_for: Seventh-day Adventist Church members seeking faith-aligned financial services from a member-owned institution
Students and employees of Adventist colleges looking for competitive auto and personal loans
Employees of Adventist hospitals wanting access to member-owned credit union products
Individuals in the Lincoln, Nebraska area seeking an alternative to for-profit banks with community focus
diagnosis: Lincoln SDA Credit Union is best suited for Seventh-day Adventist Church members, Adventist college students and employees, and Adventist hospital employees seeking competitive loan rates and member-owned financial services aligned with their faith values. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—non-Adventists cannot join, and potential members should verify their qualification status before pursuing membership or loans.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
